Foot pain

Foot pain
Toe pain
There are lots of causes of toe pain. You can usually ease the pain yourself. But sometimes it can be something serious like a broken toe.
Causes of toe pain 
A sore toe is often caused by exercising too much or wearing shoes that are too tight.
Do not try to diagnose the cause of your pain yourself.
Some of the possible causes of toe pain 
Symptoms
Possible cause
Pain or swelling around the nail, nail curls into the toe
Ingrown toe nail
Hard bony lump near the big toe
Bunion
Pain, tingling and numbness when you're cold or stressed, toes can change colour
Raynaud's or chilblains
Pain, swollen or bruised toe, hurts to walk
Sprained or broken toe
Sudden pain, stiffness, hot swollen or red skin around the toe joint - this may be harder to see on brown or black skin
Gout
Important
If you think you may have broken your toe, do not try to treat it yourself. Contact NHS 111 to find out what to do.
How to ease toe pain yourself 
You may be able to treat the pain yourself if your toe is not broken.
Do
rest and raise your foot when you can
put an ice pack (or bag of frozen peas) wrapped in a towel on your toe for up to 20 minutes every
